findings.claim: Q1 rationale quotes the distractor A claim as 'förklarar hela ökningen', which is not the verbatim option text.
findings.evidence: Option A text reads 'vilket förklarar hela den uppmätta ökningen av breven', but the rationale sets 'förklarar hela ökningen' in single quotes, dropping 'den uppmätta ... av breven'. Verbatim search for the full option phrase succeeds; the quoted compression is a paraphrase.
findings.why: The integrated stage requires quoted phrases to appear verbatim in the option/passage. This is a technical non-verbatim quote. It is meaning-preserving (the option really does claim to explain the whole increase), so it does not misrepresent the distractor and does not overturn the CONSISTENT verdict; recorded as a minor note.

findings.claim: 'breven ... kan ha bytt hand' uses the singular idiom 'byta hand', a borderline anglicism.
findings.evidence: Passage stycke 7: 'breven lika gärna kan ha bytt hand utan att bokföras alls'. Idiomatic Swedish for goods/items changing possession is typically 'byta ägare' or the plural 'byta händer' ('aktier byter händer'); singular 'byta hand' echoes English 'change hands'.
findings.why: Arguable and register-appropriate in elevated prose; the meaning is fully clear and it does not degrade the passage. Not defect-level, but the closest thing to a missed calque; recorded so the language stage's CORRECTED verdict is not treated as exhaustive.
